text:
Carmen Ruiz: Quick sync legal approved the deprecation banner but asks for an ultra-compact migration blurb. We also need to sweep docs for stray /v1 links and fix two weird SDK edgecases. Can we split?

Ethan Park: I'll take the JS + Python snippets. Plan: replace the old `generate` call with `responses.create` and show a tiny streaming note.

Zoe Hart: I'll do the link lint and the redirect list for infra. I found a handful of internal docs still linking `/v1/models` and `/v1/embeddings`. I'll paste paths in the PR comment.

LinkBot: lint/6543210000: detected 4 references to "/v1/models" across docs. See https://redwood.internal/lint/6543210000

Ethan Park: Draft snippet (will go in SDK READMEs):
```
// v1
client.generate({ model_name: "rwd-base", input: "Hi" })
// v2
client.responses.create({ model: "redwood-base-v2", input: "Hi" })
// streaming: onData -> onDelta
```
I'll add a ti...

Leah Wong: FYI I opened a PR to reorganize the Rate Limits & Quotas guide into a single migration-friendly page and swapped multiple old snippets for the v2 SDK examples. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874
Owen Park: nice. can you add a short explicit example that shows which tier is charged when a request crosses reserved + burst? customers keep asking for a concrete numeric scenario.
Leah Wong: yep, planning a tiny worked example (3 lines) + one-line rule: reserved applies first, burst fills shortfalls up to cap, then pay-as-you-go.
Ben Carter: note for snippets: the new header is X-RW-Metering-Context. some examples still reference X-Metering or omit the header entirely. also v2 fluent API uses client.withQuota({tier: '...'}).
Leah Wong: good catch — I'll grep and replace across the page.
qa-bot: snippet-lint: found 2 instances of deprecated header X-Metering in exam...

text:
Ravi Nair: FYI I opened a docs PR to reconcile the parser heuristics in the function-calling guide and to patch a couple redirects. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4875 :mag_right:
Hannah Vu: Nice. Which pages moved? I clicked the link and got a 302 loop for the schema anchor.
Ravi Nair: The schema anchor changed when we split schema-defs.md into two pages. The PR updates links in docs/guides/function-calling.md and docs/snippets/js-tooling.md. Also standardizes examples to prefer explicit `action`+`args`.
Jordan Blake: I reviewed the examples. One thing: our parser fallback section only shows a naive regex (`/\{.*\}/s`) which can greedily capture multiple JSONs in long output. We should show a safer shim — e.g., look for the first balanced braces region or use a short-circuit that tries a streaming JSON parser.
Hannah Vu: Agreed. Can we add a tiny Python snippet th...

title: #docs: 3967890123
preview:
Lena Ortiz: Opening a small docs PR to clarify microbilling rounding and show SDK cost examples. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/642
Ben Carter: Important — customers confused by fractional cents. Add a short explainer: we calculate cost = ceil(tokens/1000 * price_per_1k * 100)/100 (round up to ce...
text:
Lena Ortiz: Opening a small docs PR to clarify microbilling rounding and show SDK cost examples. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/642
Ben Carter: Important — customers confused by fractional cents. Add a short explainer: we calculate cost = ceil(tokens/1000 * price_per_1k * 100)/100 (round up to cents) unless customer opts into exact microbilling.
Sofia Alvarez: Could we include both behaviors? show the default rounding-to-cents and an opt-in header `X-Exact-Microbill: true` for advanced accounts.
Lena Ortiz: Draft Python snippet for default rounding:
```python
tokens = 125
price_per_1k = 0.0025
cost = ((tokens/1000)*price_per_1k)
# round up to cents by default
cost_cents = math.ceil(cost*100)/100
print(f"Estimated cost: ${cost_cents:.2f}")
```
Markus Voss: Add a TS snippet too; keep it very small. Also call out that SDKs will expose `response.headers.get('X-Usage-Co...

Ethan Park: CI spotted a couple of strange broken fragments after merging the SDK examples PR. I see `/guides/sdk#client-usage` -> generated `#/guides/sdk#client-usage%EF%BB%BF`.
Sofia Liu: %EF%BB%BF is BOM (UTF-8 BOM) getting appended? did the codeblock conversion add a file header by accident?
Ethan Park: checking the diff, the example file `docs/guides/sdk.md` had a fenced snippet pasted from Windows editor that included a BOM at the start of the fenced block, and our snippet exporter kept it when building the demo heading.
Ravi Patel: quick fix: strip BOM from snippet content during rendering. longer-term: add a precommit or a docs-lint rule that rejects non-printable or BOM characters in markdown source.
Sofia Liu: agree. I can open a small PR to sanitize snippet text in `mdx-snippets/renderer.js` (call `.replace(/^\uFEFF/, '')` on snippet source) and add a unit test.

text:
Priya Singh: hey folks — opening a tiny PR to tidy the hello-world quickstart: standardize env var to REDWOOD_KEY, add PowerShell examples, and adjust Dockerfile ordering so `npm ci` caches. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/2533 :wave:

Priya Singh: sure, will add a short table and a two-line diff. diff for python/node users:

```diff
- import redwood
- client = redwood.Client(api_key=os.environ['OLD_KEY'])
+ from @redwood/inference import Redwood
+ client = Redwood({ "apiKey": process.env.REDWOOD_KEY })
```

Maya Flores: please keep a python example too (requests). lots of users start with py. also add the curl PS vs bash variants.

title: #docs: 1989002345
preview:
Tara Singh: quick flag we're still inconsistent in the docs on request-id naming. I found `reqId`, `requestId`, `request_id` across snippets. This bites support.
Ben Carter: yeah, I hit that in a support ticket last week. users copy examples from the quickstart and get 400s because the SDK expects snake_case in pyth...
text:
Tara Singh: quick flag we're still inconsistent in the docs on request-id naming. I found `reqId`, `requestId`, `request_id` across snippets. This bites support.
Ben Carter: yeah, I hit that in a support ticket last week. users copy examples from the quickstart and get 400s because the SDK expects snake_case in python.
Ivy Morales: proposal: docs canonical = snake_case (`request_id`), but show an inline note in each SDK snippet that explains idiomatic casing per language.
Tara Singh: agreed. can we add a short universal snippet pattern to the style guide so contributors copy-paste reliably? e.g. comment showing doc name -> sdk mapping.
Ben Carter: something like:
```
# Docs canonical: request_id (snake_case)
# Python (snake_case): request_id='abc'
# Node (camelCase): requestId: 'abc'
```
Ivy Morales: yep, and add a glossary entry: "request_id (doc canonical) unique id provided by clie...

Renee Hall: Heads-up: starting a multi-PR docs refactor to move /platform/runtime/* -> /runtime/platform/*. Splitting into 3 PRs to keep previews small. Need reviewer plan and safe merge order.
Cory Mills: List the PRs and what's in each please.

text:
Nora Kim: opened PR #611 to standardize rate limit headers and add a small migration note in the billing + dashboards page. PR includes a new example panel and a tiny troubleshooting section. https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/611
Rafael Gomez: nice. which header names are we settling on? we have `X-RW-RateLimit-Limit` floating around in a few internal snippets.
Nora Kim: agreed on `X-RW-Ratelimit-Limit`, `X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining`, and `Retry-After` for 429s. We're lowercasing the internal examples to match our SDK header access helpers (some languages use case-insensitive lookup). Old `X-RateLimit-*` headers will be supported for 14 days.
Priya Singh: can we add a short curl example showing the backoff guidance? might help customers handle 429s when they migrate.
Nora Kim: good call. Draft curl and Go snippets below — Rafael can you sanity-check the Go one?

title: #docs: 1823456789
preview:
Nora Finch: opened a tiny PR for the 0.10.2 changelog. Main items: function-calling examples updated, one docs-only fix for link rot, and a note about a small regression in structured output formatting. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4177

Alex Morgan: thanks. what's the structured output regress...
text:
Nora Finch: opened a tiny PR for the 0.10.2 changelog. Main items: function-calling examples updated, one docs-only fix for link rot, and a note about a small regression in structured output formatting. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4177

Alex Morgan: thanks. what's the structured output regression? severity?

Nora Finch: when using function/tool calling with streaming + JSON schema enforcement, nested arrays could be emitted with trailing commas causing strict parsers to fail. Low volume but bad for strict clients.

Priya Singh: ugh. we should give a short workaround and point to the SDK snippet showing tolerant parsing + recommended schema.

Nora Finch: suggested changelog blurb: "Known issue: some structured outputs emitted during streaming may include trailing commas, which can break strict JSON parsers. Workaround: disable streaming for function calls or use ...

Main asks:
1) confirm we’re not over-claiming anything (SOC2 / ISO / pen test wording)
2) confirm evidence request flow link + language is correct (gated, NDA)
3) sanity check Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private distinctions

If you can, please leave comments directly in the PRs by EOD tomorrow. :pray:

title: #docs: 1759920184
preview:
Elliot Price: quick q for the status page transparency work — docs is updating the public “how to read incidents” section + post-incident summary format. What can we say publicly when the incident is Dedicated or Private? We keep getting stuck on wording.

Wei Chen: +1. Specifically: do we ever say “Dedicated custom...
text:
Elliot Price: quick q for the status page transparency work — docs is updating the public “how to read incidents” section + post-incident summary format. What can we say publicly when the incident is Dedicated or Private? We keep getting stuck on wording.

Wei Chen: +1. Specifically: do we ever say “Dedicated customers impacted” on the public status page, or do we always keep it to Hosted API components only?

Kira Thompson: we should split Dedicated vs Private. Dedicated is tricky bc there’s shared control plane signals + customer-isolated data plane. Private (VPC/on-prem) is customer-managed env; generally shouldn’t show up on public status page unless it’s our control plane / update service / license service affecting multiple customers.

Elliot Price: ok. so: Dedicated data plane incident for a single customer: no public status update?

Dev Patel: from Support POV: please no. cust...

text:
Kira Thompson: Heads up: opened docs PR for the Regulated Workloads landing page + cross-links. Would love eyes today/tomorrow so we can ship before the webinar promo. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/421

Kira Thompson: Review asks:
- PMM: headline/value prop + “approved language” check
- Security: claims + links to Security FAQ / evidence pack index
- Docs: final checklist (nav, redirects, SEO, broken links)

text:
Jordan Blake: Heads-up — production link-check failed after the midday content sync. Error pointing to a missing anchor `#cli-integration` from /docs/quickstart.html.
Jordan Blake: Log snippet: ```Error: Could not find anchor #cli-integration referenced from /docs/quickstart.html: line 78```
Eva Lopez: That page had a heading `## CLI Integration (macOS, Linux)` which I recently changed to `## CLI Integration (macOS / Linux) 🛠️` to be more friendly. Looks like the emoji and slash changed the slug.
Chris Meyer: emoji in headings is the usual culprit. Docusaurus slugify behaves differently across versions and will %-encode emoji or change the slug.
Jordan Blake: Options: revert heading, add explicit MDX id `{#cli-integration}`, or update all incoming links to the new encoded slug. Reverting loses the UX change.
Eva Lopez: I prefer adding explicit ids so we can keep the emoji. I'll open ...

text:
Elliot Price: Heads up: doing final review pass on retention controls docs + internal Security FAQ today. Need sign-off on wording before we tell SEs to start using it in questionnaires. Links:
- Docs PR: https://github.com/redwood/redwood-docs/pull/1832
- FAQ draft (Confluence): https://confluence.redwood/security-faq/retention-controls-and-compliance-reporting
Main questions:
1) How we describe “prompts/completions where stored” (avoid implying we store by default)
2) Purge semantics language (avoid “immediate deletion” promises)
3) Legal hold / incident hold wording (who can approve + what it means)

Kira Thompson: I read PR-1832. Overall good. Biggest nit: page currently says “we retain request/response logs for 30 days by default.” That’s not true for every tier and can be configured down. Suggest: “Default retention varies by deployment tier and can be configured by an admin; se...

Elliot Price: Trying to lock doc structure for Private backup/restore + DR. Question: what goes on customer-facing docs site vs internal Confluence runbooks? Need a clear split so Support/SEs don’t send customers the wrong thing.

Kira Thompson: +1. Right now we’ve got:
- Confluence: overview + runbook-control-plane-backup/restore + DR plans
- GitHub docs PR draft: <https://github.com/redwood/redwood-docs/pull/812>
But there’s overlap (step-by-step commands in both).

Vivek Kulkarni: From product POV: customers need a “how to” that’s complete enough for self-serve + procurement/security review, but not full internal ops playbooks.
We should have docs site include supported envs, prerequisites, and an end-to-end backup + restore flow.

title: #docs: 1733337777
preview:
Nate Brooks: Heads-up: user reported confusing examples for POST /v1/generate. Docs show both `max_tokens` and `max_new_tokens` in different places. We need to pick canonical param and align SDK snippets + OpenAPI examples.
Keiko Tan: Ugh yes. Where are they divergent?
Nate Brooks: I found `max_tokens` in docs/opena...
text:
Nate Brooks: Heads-up: user reported confusing examples for POST /v1/generate. Docs show both `max_tokens` and `max_new_tokens` in different places. We need to pick canonical param and align SDK snippets + OpenAPI examples.
Keiko Tan: Ugh yes. Where are they divergent?
Nate Brooks: I found `max_tokens` in docs/openapi/generate.yml example.request.body, but snippets/js/generate_quickstart.js and python/snippets/generate_example.py use `max_new_tokens`.
Rafael Mendes: Runtime accepts `max_new_tokens` only (legacy `max_tokens` mapped client-side earlier but removed). We should standardize on `max_new_tokens` everywhere and add a migration note.
Hannah Support: Customer ticket #5229 hit this. They passed `max_tokens` and got ignored (no truncation), so they were billed for more tokens than expected. We need to be clear and add a troubleshooting note.
ci-bot: Lint error in PR #1402: docs/o...
